name: Kostroma Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the central field representing the Volga River

description: A vertical tricolour with a wide central blue stripe flanked by two narrower red stripes, charged in the centre with a golden galley.
history: Adopted on 19 October 2000. The galley device recalls Empress Catherine the Great's visit to Kostroma by ship down the Volga.

features.role: imperial galley from the Kostroma Oblast coat of arms
features.symbolism: a golden galley recalling the ship on which Empress Catherine the Great sailed down the Volga to Kostroma; taken from the oblast's coat of arms.
